relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: The incense altar is made from acacia wood.
Its square form and horns are measured.
Gold covers the altar of fragrance.
Rings prepare it for carrying.
Gold-covered poles are made.
It stands near the veil before the meeting place.
Morning incense rises with the tending of lamps.
Evening incense rises as the lamps are raised.
The altar keeps its appointed purpose.
Yearly atonement marks the incense altar as most holy.
YHWH speaks again.
Counting lives requires ransom before YHWH.
Each counted one gives the half-shekel.
Those twenty and upward give the contribution.
Rich do not add and poor do not diminish: life-value is equal.
The silver becomes memorial and service of the tent.
YHWH speaks again.
A bronze basin is made for washing.
Hands and feet are washed.
Approach to tent and altar is prepared with water.
Washing becomes an age-long statute.
YHWH speaks again.
Chief spices are gathered.
Cassia and olive oil complete the measure.
The perfumer makes holy anointing oil.
Tent and ark are anointed.
Table, lampstand, and incense altar are anointed.
Burnt altar, vessels, basin, and stand are anointed.
The holy things become most holy.
Aaron and sons are anointed for priestly service.
The oil remains holy through generations.
The holy mixture is not ordinary perfume.
Imitation and misuse rupture the holy boundary.
Incense spices are gathered in equal parts.
The perfumer makes pure holy incense.
Fine incense rests before testimony where YHWH meets.
The incense formula belongs to YHWH.
Holy fragrance is not made for private consumption.

contemplative_note: Exodus 30 tends the hidden senses of worship: fragrance, washing, oil, equal life-value, and the refusal to turn holy presence into private possession.
